entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method that uses genomic information to predict the genetic value of individuals to accelerate genetic gain.

entities.name: PHENOTYPIC SELECTION
entities.description: A breeding method based on the observable characteristics of plants to select for desired traits.

entities.name: INTERCROPPING
entities.description: An agricultural practice where two or more component crops are grown together on the same piece of land.

entities.name: GENETIC GAIN
entities.description: The increase in the average genetic value of a trait within a population over generations.

entities.name: STOCHASTIC SIMULATION
entities.description: A computational method used to model random variables to compare different breeding strategies.

entities.name: MONOCROP GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced by a crop when grown as a single species.

entities.name: INTERCROP GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain produced by a crop when grown in an intercropping system.

entities.name: GENETIC CORRELATION
entities.description: The degree to which two traits are controlled by the same set of genes.

entities.name: GENERATION INTERVAL
entities.description: The average time between the selection of parents and the selection of their offspring.

entities.name: SOIL STRUCTURE
entities.description: The physical arrangement of soil particles, which can be enhanced by intercropping.

entities.name: FERTILITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to provide essential nutrients for plant growth.

entities.name: WEED SUPPRESSION
entities.description: The ability of a cropping system to inhibit the growth of weeds.

entities.name: PESTS
entities.description: Organisms that cause damage to crops.

entities.name: DISEASES
entities.description: Pathological conditions that affect crop health and yield.

entities.name: SUBSISTENCE AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Farming practiced primarily to provide food for the farmer and their family.
